title: markdown-html design-system customization config
description: JSON schema for the design-system config written by onboard.py and consumed by every markdown-html converter via config_loader.py. Lives at ~/.config/markdown-html/design-system.json (global) or ./.markdown-html/design-system.json (project).

version.description: Schema version. Bump on breaking changes to the layout.

default_output_dir.description: Where converters save generated HTML by default. Must be a writable path. The orchestrator's output_path_resolver.py also accepts a --out override per conversion.

accent.description: Optional accent color. If null/empty, brand_palette_validator derives it from the primary via hue-shift + lighten.

bg.description: Optional background override. If null, derived from primary.

text.description: Optional body text override. If null, derived (off-white on dark bg, near-black on light bg).

heading_font.description: Google Font family for headings. e.g., Inter, Source Serif 4, Playfair Display.

body_font.description: Google Font family for body text.

scale_ratio.description: Modular type-scale ratio. 1.25 = major third (default), 1.333 = perfect fourth, 1.5 = perfect fifth.

design_style.description: Layout density preset consumed by every converter. editorial = magazine-like with wide margins and pull-quotes; technical = docs-like with sticky TOC and code emphasis; minimal = sparse with maximum whitespace; playful = product-marketing with color blocks and varied scale.

code_theme.description: Prism.js theme selection. auto = follows prefers-color-scheme.

max_depth.description: Deepest heading level included in the TOC.

company_name.description: Optional, shown in footer of every generated HTML.

logo_url.description: Optional. Base64-embedded at render time by default; pass --logo-mode link to inline the URL instead.

derived_palette.description: 12 CSS custom properties derived from the brand input by brand_palette_validator.derive_palette(). Stored here so every converter has identical tokens without re-deriving. Keys are CSS variable names; values are HEX or rgba() strings.

setup_completed_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp written by onboard.py on successful completion. The orchestrator refuses to convert if this is null.
